name: Le Petibonum
description: Beachfront dining with creative Caribbean cuisine. Famous for its casual yet gourmet atmosphere right on the sand.

name: Habitation Clément
description: A historic rum distillery and botanical gardens. Birthplace of Rhum Agricole and home to permanent contemporary art exhibitions.

name: Jardin de Balata
description: Spectacular botanical garden with treetop walks. Discover thousands of tropical plant species in a lush mountain setting.

name: Ti' Sable
description: Upscale beach restaurant with amazing sunsets. Perfect for a long lunch or a romantic dinner by the turquoise waters.

name: Montagne Pelée Hike
description: The highest peak in Martinique. A challenging hike through cloud forests with breathtaking crater views.

name: Plage des Salines
description: Often cited as the most beautiful beach in Martinique. Fine white sand, coconut trees, and crystal clear water.

name: Cascade Didier
description: Adventurous hike through tunnels and rivers leading to two hidden waterfalls in the tropical forest.

name: Le Zandoli
description: Creative and refined cuisine at La Suite Villa. Panoramic view of the Bay of Fort-de-France.

name: Presqu'île de la Caravelle
description: Nature reserve offering diverse landscapes from dry forests to mangroves and stunning coastal cliffs.

name: Chez Carole
description: Authentic Creole flavors in the heart of the capital's central market. A must-visit for foodies.

name: La Savane des Esclaves
description: Open-air museum dedicated to the history of Martinique, slavery, and traditional Amerindian living.

name: Piliers du Sud
description: Excellent seafood restaurant overlooks the marina. Fresh lobster and great cocktails.

name: Madiana Cinéma Multiplex
description: The premier 10-screen cinema multiplex and convention center of Martinique, featuring modern projection, ultra-comfy seating, and hot classic popcorn.

name: Cinéma Le Rex
description: A beautifully historic movie house in downtown Fort-de-France, offering boutique art-house films, classic retrospectives, and local indie blockbusters.

name: Plaza Bowling d'Acajou
description: A top-tier entertainment center with multiple glowing neon-lit lanes, arcade games, billiards tables, and a tropical fluid cocktail bar.

name: Acro'Kart
description: An sensational outdoor go-karting complex at the historic Village de la Poterie in Les Trois-Îlets. Boasting a twisting 600m speedway, rapid Sodikarts, and electronic timing for racers of all levels.

name: Laser West Laser Tag
description: An immersive glow-in-the-dark laser tag labyrinth with futuristic sci-fi obstacles, neon lighting, and interactive team scoreboards.

name: Hippopotamus Steakhouse
description: A stylish and modern French-inspired family steakhouse. Renowned for its select choice steak cuts, perfectly grilled ribs, appetizing burgers, and high-quality Parisian-style service.

name: Tori Sushi
description: An upscale, contemporary Japanese sushi lounge and bar. Precision-crafted sushi platters, fresh sashimi, poke bowls, and unique gourmet Asian-Creole fusion styles await.

name: McDonald's
description: The golden-arches corner in Le Lamentin, serving familiar, highly-consistent fast food classics, crispy hot fries, Big Macs, and the signature French-themed local McBaguettes.

name: Bokay Burger (BKB)
description: The beloved custom local standard 'BKB' (Bokay Burger)! Passionate about showcasing artisanal, fresh beef/chicken patties on brioche bun options drizzled with sweet island Creole BBQ glazes and signature fries.

name: Chez Fredo
description: An absolute monument of Southern Martinique culture. Chez Fredo is the warm, energetic beach shack where travellers find authentic spicy codfish accras, refreshing local punches, and regular live Creole music sessions.

name: Le Balaou
description: A delightful beachside terrace in Sainte-Luce serving ocean-to-table seafood choices, garlic grilled spiny lobster, traditional lambi and stunning sunset beach vistas.

name: La Dunette
description: An spectacular waterfront cocktail lounge, hotel, and restaurant at the tip of Sainte-Anne with a famous open-air wooden pier stretching into crystal clear waters. Celebrated for island tapas, rum cocktails, and gorgeous twilight vibes.
